cations and electrons (particles in sodium) (1)  Na + and Cl − /cations and anions (particles in sodium chloride) (1) Allow giant ionic / (giant) lattice Ignore metal Ignore ion(s) Ignore electrostatic attr actions in M2 and M3 Accept London / dispersion (forces) Allow van der Waals’ (forces) Ignore weak (forces) Allow positive ions and electrons Allow sodium atoms / ions and electrons Allow positive (sodium) ion and ne gative (chloride / chlorine) ion Ignore just sodium ions and chloride ions Penalise incorrect charge on an ion once only e.g. Na 2+ Example of table: Substance Sodium Sodium chloride Chlorine Melting temperature / o C (98) (801) ( − 101) Type of structure (giant) giant (simple molecular) Type of bond or force broken on melting metallic ionic intermolecular forces Particles involved Na + and electrons / cations and electrons Na + and Cl − /cations and anions (chlorine molecules) Question number Additional guidance Mark  correct dot-and-cross diagram Example of dot-and-cross diagram: Allow any combination of dots and crosses, including all dots or all crosses Allow overlapping circles Allow electrons in bonds al ong the axis of the bond Ignore missing bracket and charge Ignore lines representi ng covalent bonds e.g. x • Question number Additional guidance Mark  Shape – tetrahedral (1)  Justification – (four) bonding pairs /pairs of electrons (around P) (1)  (electron pairs) arranged to minimise repulsion (1) Stand alone No TE on (c)(i) for shape Allow the number of electron pairs shown in (c)(i) Allow regions of electron density for electron pairs Ignore reference to lone pa ir-lone pair / lone pair- bond pair repulsion Allow (electron pairs) arranged for maximum separation / as far apart as possible Ignore electron pair s repel equally Penalise use of bonds for el

 (alkene is) C H Allow H C Allow large numbers e.g. C8H16 Do not award C H Question number Additional guidance Mark  structure of C H branched alkene Example of structure: Allow any unambiguous structure e.g. structural or displayed formula or any combination of these / skeletal formula Ignore name even if incorrect Question number Additional guidance Mark  structure of one geometric isomer and name (1)  structure of the other geometric isomer and name (1) Examples of struct ures and names: and trans -but-2-ene / E -but-2-ene and cis- but-2-ene / Z -but-2-ene Allow isomers in either order Allow 2-butene for but-2-ene Allow any unambiguous structures e.g. displayed formulae or skeletal formulae Ignore missing hyphens If no other mark is scor ed, allow (1) for two correct structures or two correct names Question number Additional guidance Mark  skeletal formul a of product Example of skeletal formula: Ignore structural / displayed formul a Question number Additional guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to one of the following pairs: Either  steam / H O(g) (1)  phosphoric((V)) acid (catalyst) / H PO (1) Or  (concentrated) sulfuric acid / H SO (1)  followed by water / H O (1) Allow reagent and condition written on either dotted line for the steam and phosphoric acid answer Allow water / H O and heat / any temperature above 100 o C Ignore pressure If oxidation number is give n, it must be correct Allow just ‘acid catalyst’ Ignore hydrochloric acid / just ‘H + ’ Ignore specified temperat ure / heat / reflux Do not award H O(g) Question number Additional guidance Mark

 curly arrow from C=C bond to / towards I δ + and curly arrow from I-Cl bond to, or just beyond Cl (1)  intermediate (1)  lone pair on Cl − and curly arrow from lone pair to carbon with positive charge (1)  structure of major product (1) Example of mechanism: Do not award δ + charge on intermediate Do not award δ− charge on chloride ion Allow curly arrow from lone pair to positive charge Note Mechanism for the formation of the minor product can score M1, M3 and M4 Question number Additional guidance Mark

parts.text: 23 (e) When 6.95 g of FeSO 4 .xH 2 O is heated, 2.00 g of iron(III) oxide, 0.80 g of sulfur dioxide and 1.00 g of sulfur trioxide are produced. The only other product is water. Deduce the overall equation for the reaction using these data. State symbols are not required. Y ou must show your working. [A r values: H = 1.0 O = 16.0 S = 32.1 Fe =
parts.answer:  pent-2-ene Allow 2-pentene Ignore E / Z / cis / trans Do not award j ust ‘pentene’ Question number Additional guidance Mark

 conversion of volume to m (1)  rearrangement of ideal gas equation (1)  evaluation to give n (1)  deduction of number of double bonds (1) Example of calculation: volume of H = 600 = 6 x 10 − / 0.0006 m 1 x 10 n = pV RT or n = 1.24 x 10 x 6 x 10 − 8.31 x 298 TE on volume n = 0.03004 / 0.0300 / 0.030 / 0.03 TE on volume ratio alkene : H = 0.01 : 0.03 / 1 : 3 and so there are 3 double bonds TE on volume Final answer with no working scores (1) I g nore SF includin g 1SF (Total for Question 23 = 16 marks) Question number Additional guidance Mark
 all three numbers correct Example of table: Number of protons Number of neutrons Number of electrons 26 30 24 Question number Additional guidance Mark

 expression to calculate rela tive atomic mass (1)  correct answer to 3SF (1) Example of calculation: (54 x 5.84) + (56 x 91.68) + (57 x 2.17) + (58 x 0.31) 100 Relative atomic ma ss (= 55.911) = 55.9 TE on incorrect numbers in correct expression Ignore units of g mol − or g mol − Do not award other inco rrect units e.g. g or % Correct answer with some working scores (2) Question number Additional guidance Mark

 ionic equation (1)  all state symbols (1) Example of equation: Mg(s) + Fe 2+ (aq) → Fe(s) + Mg 2+ (aq) Allow multiples State symbols conditional on correct equation Allow state symbols if equation includes correct metals combined w ith ions with incorrect charges e.g. 3Mg(s) + 2Fe 3+ (aq) → 2Fe(s) + 3Mg 2+ (aq) Or 2Mg(s) + Fe 2+ (aq) → Fe(s) + 2Mg + (aq) Allow state symbols for ba lanced non-ionic equation Mg(s) + FeSO → Fe(s) + MgSO (aq) or multiples Question number Additional guidance Mark

 calculation of mass of oxygen and working to find mol (1)  calculation of mol of Fe, S and O (1)  calculation of simplest whole number ratio and deduction of empirical formula (1) Example of calculation: mass of oxygen = 25.00 – 6.98 – 6.03 = 11.99 (g) Fe : S : O mol 6.98 : 6.03 : 11.99 55.8 32.1 16.0 = 0.12509 : 0.18785 : 0.74938 Ignore SF except 1 SF in M2 ratio 1 : 1.5 : 6 = 2 : 3 : 12 and empirical formula is Fe S O TE on mol Fe, S and O Allow symbols in any order Correct empirical formula with no working scores (3) Penalise incorrect rounding / truncation of numbers once only in M2 e.g. 0.12 / 0.18 / 0.74 Note Allow (3) for correct working with Fe (SO ) but Fe (SO ) with no working scores (0) Question number Additional guidance Mark

 calculation of mol of iron(III) oxi de (1)  calculation of mol of sulfur dioxi de and mol of sulfur trioxide (1)  calculation of mass and mol of H O (1)  calculation of value of x (1)  balanced equation (1) Example of calculation: mol Fe O = 2.00 = 0.012531 / 1.2531 x 10 − 159.6 mol SO = 0.80 = 0.0124805 / 1.24805 x 10 − 64.1 and mol SO = 1.00 = 0.012484 / 1.2484 x 10 − 80.1 mass of H O = 6.95 – (2.00 + 0.80 + 1.00) = 3.15 (g) and mol of H O = 3.15 = 0.175 (mol) 18 Ratio SO : SO : H O = 1 :1 : 14 There must be 2FeSO to produce SO and SO So x = 7 TE on M1, M2, and M3 This mark may be awarded in M5 Example of equation: 2FeSO .7H O → Fe O + SO + SO + 14H O Stand alone mark Allow multiples Allow fractions for numbers of moles TE on value of x in M4 provi ded equation is balanced Ignore state symbols even if incorrect See next page for alternative methods Alternative metho d s for M3 and M4: Method 1 mol FeSO = 2 x 1.2531 x 10 − = 0.025062 (1) M r of hydrate = 6.95 / 0.025062 = 277.305 and mass of water = 265.34 − 151.9 = 125.405 (g) and mol water = 125.405/18 = 6.9669 = 7 (1) Method 2 mass of water = 6.95 – (2.00 + 0.80 + 1.00) = 3.15 (g) and mass of FeSO = 3.8(0) (g) (1) FeSO H O mol FeSO and water 3.80 3.15 151.9 18 = 0.025 0.175 simplest ratio 1 7 (1) (Total for Question 24 = 13 marks) Pearson Education Limited.
